name: Monsanto-Eduardo VII Park Green Corridor Trail
name_original: Do Parque Eduardo VII à Serra de Monsanto — percurso em Lisboa
description: Hike from Eduardo VII Park to Serra de Monsanto in Lisbon, enjoying city views and dense forests on this 5.3-mile moderate route.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Coração De Jesus offer urban exploration through historic districts and green spaces. The area features routes with moderate elevation changes, providing views of city landmarks and architectural highlights. These trails are primarily paved or well-maintained paths, suitable for accessible walks within a city environment. Best easy hiking trails around Coração De Jesus The most popular easy hiking route is Liberty Avenue – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Avenida, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This route connects prominent avenues with historic viewpoints. Another top favourite among local hikers is Príncipe Real Garden – Ascensor / Elevador da Glória loop from Marquês de Pombal, an easy 2.9 miles (4.7 km) path. It features city gardens and iconic funiculars. Local hikers also love the Rua Augusta – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Avenida, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through central pedestrian areas, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Hiking around Coração De Jesus is defined by urban landscapes, historic architecture, and city parks. The network offers options for various ability levels, focusing on accessible and enjoyable walks. The routes in Coração De Jesus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 580 reviews. More than 5,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Coração De Jesus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Coração De Jesus?
A: There are over 20 easy hiking trails around Coração De Jesus, offering a variety of urban explorations through historic districts and green spaces. In total, the area features 26 routes for various ability levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in Coração De Jesus?
A: Easy hikes in Coração De Jesus primarily feature urban landscapes, historic architecture, and city parks. The trails are generally paved or well-maintained paths, suitable for accessible walks within a city environment. You can expect moderate elevation changes as you explore.

Q: Are there any family-friendly easy hikes in Coração De Jesus?
A: Yes, many of the easy hikes in Coração De Jesus are suitable for families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10346502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Príncipe Real Garden – Ascensor / Elevador da Glória loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt;, which features city gardens and iconic funiculars, are great options for an enjoyable family outing.

Q: What are some popular easy loop trails in Coração De Jesus?
A: Popular easy loop trails include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32233675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Liberty Avenue – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Avenida&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.0 km trail connecting prominent avenues with historic viewpoints. Another favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32379494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rua Augusta – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Avenida&lt;/a&gt;, leading through central pedestrian areas.

Q: What can I see along the easy hiking trails in Coração De Jesus?
A: Along the easy hiking trails, you can explore various city landmarks and architectural highlights. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/399846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Overseas Combatants&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8178196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eduardo VII Park Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Christ the King&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter beautiful green spaces like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gulbenkian Garden&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints on the easy hikes in Coração De Jesus?
A: Yes, several easy hikes offer excellent viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32233675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Liberty Avenue – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Avenida&lt;/a&gt; provides views from the Rua Augusta Arch. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8178196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eduardo VII Park Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is also a popular spot for panoramic city views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Coração De Jesus?
A: The komoot community highly rates the easy hikes in Coração De Jesus, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 580 reviews. Hikers often praise the urban exploration opportunities, the historic architecture, and the well-maintained paths that make for accessible and enjoyable walks.

Q: How long do easy hikes in Coração De Jesus typically take?
A: Easy hikes in Coração De Jesus vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32233675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Liberty Avenue – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Avenida&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.0 km, typically takes about 1 hour 5 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18945698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Justa Lift – Baixa district loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt;, at 6.0 km, can take around 1 hour 40 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or architectural highlights to look out for?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in architectural and historical landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/399846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Overseas Combatants&lt;/a&gt;, the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Christ the King&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6947921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alameda Luminous Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass by historic squares and buildings.

Q: Can I find green spaces or gardens along the easy trails?
A: Yes, Coração De Jesus offers several green spaces and gardens. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10346502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Príncipe Real Garden – Ascensor / Elevador da Glória loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt; takes you through beautiful city gardens. Other notable green areas include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gulbenkian Garden&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/853498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;University of Lisbon Botanical Garden&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that feature iconic funiculars or lifts?
A: Yes, you can experience the city's unique transport on some easy hikes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10346502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Príncipe Real Garden – Ascensor / Elevador da Glória loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt; features iconic funiculars. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18945698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Justa Lift – Baixa district loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt;, highlights the famous Santa Justa Lift.
tips: In the center of the square stands a monument to the Marquis of Pombal, one of the key figures in the history of Lisbon and all of Portugal. On the monument you can see a large figure of the marquis who was the governor of Lisbon between 1750 and 1777, along with a lion, the classic symbol of power.

The Arial is huge and totally impressive. It's hard to resist a certain magic. No wonder that festivals etc. take place here. are held frequently. The view of the city, which you ideally have “hiked” beforehand, is also magically beautiful.

Parque Eduardo VII
This famous park is named after British King Edward VII who visited the city in the twentieth century. Parque Eduardo VII is the largest park in the center of Lisbon. The park is built against a hill.

On the Planalto Trail in the Monsanto Forest Park in Lisbon, you can find a series of wooden sculptures that are part of a very special visual and natural experience.
The sculptures are located in the cypress forest area, part of the Percurso Volta do Planalto, a circular trail of approximately 7 km.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the hike from Eduardo VII Park?
faqs.answer: You can find parking options around Eduardo VII Park, particularly in nearby streets or underground parking garages. Given its central Lisbon location, public transport is also a convenient way to reach the starting point.

faqs.question: Is the route from Eduardo VII Park to Serra de Monsanto dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. Serra de Monsanto is a large urban park where dogs are permitted, though it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in more populated areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summers can be hot, so if hiking then, start early in the morning to avoid the midday heat. Winter offers cooler temperatures but can be wet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Serra de Monsanto?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this route or to access Parque Florestal de Monsanto. It is a public park, freely accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Is the hike from Eduardo VII Park to Serra de Monsanto suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as a moderate hike requiring good fitness due to its length of approximately 8.5 km (5.3 miles) and around 170 meters (558 feet) of elevation gain, the paths are generally accessible. Beginners with a reasonable level of fitness should be able to complete it, but be prepared for the duration and some uphill sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. It starts with paved or well-maintained paths in Eduardo VII Park. As you transition into Serra de Monsanto, you'll encounter a mix of tarmac paths, forest trails, and gravel sections. Expect some hills and natural obstacles like tree roots on less-paved forest paths.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints and landmarks along this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll start near the Marquis of Pombal Square and enjoy panoramic city views from the Eduardo VII Park Viewpoint. Within Monsanto, you'll pass the Miradouro do Moinho das Três Cruzes and discover unique Wooden Sculptures on the Planalto Trail, Parque Florestal de Monsanto.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in Monsanto Forest Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes and intersects with several official trails within Monsanto Forest Park and the surrounding urban green spaces. Notably, it follows significant portions of the Corredor Verde Monsanto-Parque Eduardo VII, Volta do Planalto, Montado de Monsanto, and Rota da Água, among others.

faqs.question: Are there facilities or places to get water along the route?
faqs.answer: While there are no dedicated water stations directly on the forest trails, you'll find cafes and facilities near Eduardo VII Park at the start. Within Monsanto, it's advisable to carry enough water and snacks, as amenities are sparse once you're deep in the park.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique compared to other Lisbon hikes?
faqs.answer: This trail stands out for its seamless transition from the formal urban green space of Eduardo VII Park to the expansive, wilder Monsanto Forest Park, Lisbon's 'green lung.' It offers stunning panoramic city views, utilizes the dedicated 'Corredor Verde de Monsanto,' and features diverse scenery, including unique artistic installations like the wooden sculptures within the cypress forest.

faqs.question: Where does this specific route begin?
faqs.answer: This route officially begins at Eduardo VII Park, a prominent urban park in central Lisbon. You can start from the top of the park near the Marquis of Pombal Square, which offers a downhill start through the park before heading towards Monsanto.

waypoints.name: Miradouro do Moinho das Três Cruzes
Wooden Sculptures on the Planalto Trail, Parque Florestal de Monsanto
